segments.text: Then Eliphaz the Temanite replied:
If someone ventures a word with you, will you be impatient? But who can keep from speaking?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Think how you have instructed many, how you have strengthened feeble hands.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Your words have supported those who stumbled; you have strengthened faltering knees.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: But now trouble comes to you, and you are discouraged; it strikes you, and you are dismayed.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Should not your piety be your confidence and your blameless ways your hope?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Consider now: Who, being innocent, has ever perished? Where were the upright ever destroyed?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: As I have observed, those who plow evil and those who sow trouble reap it.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: At the breath of God they perish; at the blast of his anger they are no more.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: The lions may roar and growl, yet the teeth of the great lions are broken.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: The lion perishes for lack of prey, and the cubs of the lioness are scattered.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: A word was secretly brought to me, my ears caught a whisper of it.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Amid disquieting dreams in the night, when deep sleep falls on people,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: fear and trembling seized me and made all my bones shake.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: A spirit glided past my face, and the hair on my body stood on end.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: It stopped, but I could not tell what it was. A form stood before my eyes, and I heard a hushed voice:
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Can a mortal be more righteous than God? Can even a strong man be more pure than his Maker?
segments.speaker: Divine Voice

segments.text: If God places no trust in his servants, if he charges his angels with error,
segments.speaker: Divine Voice

segments.text: how much more those who live in houses of clay, whose foundations are in the dust, who are crushed more readily than a moth!
segments.speaker: Divine Voice

segments.text: Between dawn and dusk they are broken to pieces; unnoticed, they perish forever.
segments.speaker: Divine Voice

segments.text: Are not the cords of their tent pulled up, so that they die without wisdom?
segments.speaker: Divine Voice
